finding: trusted_hosts is the union of ALL the user's registered MCP-server hosts (not scoped to the emitting server_id), so a compromised/injected external server can emit a resource_link whose host matches a DIFFERENT registered private server → MCP_USER (port-ignored, attacker-chosen path) read-SSRF against internal ports/paths — a confused deputy broader than 'same-host own-artifact'.

The feature routes external links through MCP_USER while claiming 'IMDS/link-local blocked', but MCP_USER.allow_private=true and is_blocked_v6 treats fc00::/7 as merely private, so the IPv6 ULA metadata endpoint fd00:ec2::254 is reachable — the stated IMDS guarantee is imprecise.
Policy precedence, host_of/trusted_hosts_from_urls, mapping, and all call-site arg positions verified correct; no defect.
All fetch/validate/build/ingest failures log+continue; workflow list_accessible error degrades to empty trusted set (safe, more restrictive); no new production unwrap/panic.

TEST-8 sets ZIEE_MCP_RESOURCE_LINK_ALLOW_PRIVATE via in-process set_var guarded only by serial(repos,file_storage), which does NOT serialize against the rest of the multi-threaded integration binary → data-race UB with concurrent env reads and leaks into concurrently-spawned TestServer subprocesses (harness does not env_clear), silently relaxing their SSRF policy.

Unit test allow_private_env_reads_the_flag calls set_var/remove_var with no serial guard in the multi-threaded lib-test binary — a data race against other lib tests that read/write env, the 'single-threaded within this test' comment notwithstanding.
start_fixed_response_mock moves the TcpListener into a spawned accept loop that is never cancelled, leaking a live accept task per TEST-6..9 for the remaining test-process lifetime.

The off-host redirect test uses an unreachable RFC1918 target (10.9.9.9:9), so outcome.saved==0 holds whether redirects are disabled OR followed-to-unreachable (and a 302 fails is_success anyway) — the assertion does not prove redirect-disabling and would stay green if the fix were reverted.

The workflow-site list_accessible DB query runs per tool-result-with-links even when the emitter is_built_in (already known), where persist_links never consults trusted_hosts — a wasted round-trip on the common built-in (code_sandbox ziee://) producer path.

All 14 call sites (3 prod + 11 test) place trusted_hosts in the correct positional slot; type &[String] vs &[PathBuf] prevents a silent swap; user-scoping correct; no external-crate callers; no authz bypass.

The scoped path builds via validated_client_builder(policy).redirect(Policy::none()).build() but omits .no_proxy(), unlike the mirrored discover.rs (which pairs no_proxy with credential-carrying requests), even though this fetch carries the external server's Authorization headers.

trusted_hosts was derived from accessible_servers, which is augmented with non-group-gated built-in servers via get_any_server (url NOT redacted), leaking the built-in loopback host 127.0.0.1 into the trust set so an external server's resource_link at http://127.0.0.1:<port> is fetched under MCP_USER, bypassing the default loopback block.
Second chat site had the identical loopback-leak defect via the augmented accessible_servers built-in url.

host_of normalizes both sides identically (punycode/case/IPv4-encodings/IPv6-brackets); mismatch fails closed to the stricter Public policy; credentials-in-URL hard-rejected; the final SSRF decision is still enforced by validate_outbound_url on the same URL; no panic; header construction unchanged.
No new log line emits secrets/JWT/Authorization/env-value/trusted_hosts; the SSRF-reject is still logged with the URL (symptom stays debuggable); every failure path logs, none silently no-ops.

All helpers/variants used; no orphaned const/import after the test rework (redirect response built inline); MockGuard + impl-Into<String> mock refactor sound; workflow built-in guard correct; doc claims match url_validator.rs.
